info.title: mynumbers-number
info.description:
BICS MyNumbers API makes it easy to order ready to use Cloud Numbers (IBN), International Freephone Numbers (ITFS) and Global Mobile Numbers (GMN). You can use straight away the numbers that you order through the API. The method _/order/bulk_ allows you to order multiple numbers in many countries around the world. You just have to call the service by specifying the product, the quantity, the country, the routing details and eventually, the location, the area code and the address reference. You can order a single number by calling the method _/order_. The contract of this method is the same as for the method _/order/bulk_, the only difference being that the attribute quantity must be omitted. If you want to see the numbers before ordering them, it is also possible thanks to the method _/availablenumbers_. You need to specify as input the product, the country and eventually the location and the area code. The method retrieves a list of 10 available numbers matching your input criteria. You can then order the numbers you like the most by calling the method _/order/bulk_. You must specify in the request of this method the list of numbers you want to buy. MyNumbers API exposes a method _/numbers_ that gives you the list of numbers of your inventory. You can also filter your inventory by product, country, location, area code and status. If you need the details of a specific number of your inventory including pricing and routing information, you can query the method _/numbers/{number}_. It is also possible to update in real time routing configuration numbers in bulk. You can call the method _/routes_ by filling in the list of numbers, its new number routing rule and/or its new Point of Presence (POP). MyNumbers API updates the numbers routes in real time. You are able to check our stock by calling the method _/stock/product/{product}/country/{country}/quantity/{quantity}_. This method is useful if you would like to know if there are enough numbers in stock before ordering numbers in bulk. Additionally the API allows you to query the current active price list for a given product in order to know our tariff for every country BICS offers. Finally, the API methods _/reference/locations_ shows the list of all locations and area codes where BICS offers Cloud Numbers and _/reference/pops_ shows the list of your Points of Presence (POP).

What are the errors that MyNumbers API can return ?

| Code | Description | Http response | | --- | --- | --- | | E001 | The product does not exist in our system. | 400 | | E002 | The country does not exist in our system. | 400 | | E003 | The Location (Area Code) does not exist in the Country for Product. | 400 | | E004 | The access area does not exist in the country. | 400 | | E005 | The profile {profileName} does not exist for the country. | 400 | | E006 | There is no price defined for this product and origin. | 400 | | E007 | The routing details are not valid. Please use valid access types and fill out routing details for every access type. | 400 | | E008 | The routing details are not valid. Please use valid access network and fill out routing details for every access network. | 400 | | E009 | The routing details are not valid. Please correct the CRN type. | 400 | | E010 | The routing details are not valid. Only digits and characters [A-E] are accepted for the CRN value. CRN type value is case sensitive. | 400 | | E011 | The routing details are not valid. The length of CRN value is too long. Please correct the CRN value. | 400 | | E012 | The routing details are not valid. The POP does not exist. The list of your POPs is {list of PoPs}. | 400 | | E013 | There is a problem in the routing configuration. Please contact BICS. | 400 | | E014 | There is no number in our stock for the given origin. Please contact BICS to carry on and complete with your order. | 404 | | E016 | The routing details are not valid. The CRN must start with prefix {IRN_REPLACE_PREFIX}. Please correct the CRN type or CRN value. | 400 | | E017 | The routing details are not valid. POP needs to be specified. The list of your POPs is {list of PoPs}. | 400 | | E018 | The product is not yet handled via our API. | 400 | | E019 | The routing details are not valid. CRN Parameter must be filled out for the given CRN type. | 400 | | E020 | Please fill out all the mandatory attributes. | 400 | | E021 | The routing details are not valid. At least one CRN Type must be different than NA. | 400 | | E022 | Your price list needs to be defined in our system. Please contact BICS. | 404 | | E023 | The format of the quantity is invalid. The quantity must be a positive number. | 400 | | E024 | You are not allowed to query the stock with this quantity. For big order, please contact BICS. | 400 | | E028 | The Location can not be filled out for the given Product. | 400 | | E030 | The service is temporarily unavailable. Please retry later or contact BICS. | 500 | | E031 | The media type in your header is invalid. Please use application/json. | 400 | | E033 | There is no address with this reference in our system. Please correct the reference. | 400 | | E035 | The length of the attribute {attribute} is too long. The maximum length for this attribute is {size} characters. | 400 | | E036 | An address is required to order a number in the country. Please fill in the attribute addressReference. | 400 | | E037 | According to Specifications. The following field(s) are required: VATNumber and/or Phone number. | 400 | | E039 | Address is not located in the same country as the number. Please fill out the reference of address(es) located in the same country. | 400 | | E045 | The Address is not located in the same geographical zone as the number. Please fill out the reference of an address located in the same geographical zone. | 400 | | E049 | MyNumbers team needs to verify that the address is in the same geographical zone as the number. Please contact BICS. | 400 | | E050 | The address with this reference belongs to a geographical zone where there is no number in stock. Please contact BICS to carry on and complete with your orders. | 404 | | E056 | The status value in invalid, should be Active or Deactivated. | 400 | | E057 | The Quantity is invalid. Only positive integer is accepted. | 400 | | E058 | You are not allowed to order numbers with this quantity. For big order, please contact BICS. | 400 | | E059 | The Location (Area Code) cannot be specified without country. | 400 | | E060 | The number specified in request does not exist. | 404 | | E061 | Enum value is specified incorrectly. | 400 | | E062 | Requested number can not be ordered. | 400 | | E063 | The Location (Area Code) that is specified in the order does not match to the Location (Area Code) of the number. | 400 | | E064 | The country, which specified in the order, doesn't match to the country of access number. | 400 | | E065 | The product, which specified in the order, doesn't match to the product of access number. | 400 | | E066 | There is no price defined for the number. | 400 | | E067 | Product and Country are mandatory if Location (Area Code) is requested. | 400 | | E068 | Quantity in order is not equal to amount of specified numbers in the request. | 400 | | E069 | Please specify all numbers in the request. | 400 | | E071 | This number is specified several times in the request. Please specify every number once only. | 400 | | E072 | Cannot update routing of disconnected number. | 400 | | E073 | Requested amount of numbers for updating exceeds limit of {max_amount}. | 400 | | E076 | You are not allowed to use the pattern {pattern} in the CRN value. Please provide another CRN value. | 400 | | E077 | Routing update is not allowed as there is already a routing update in progress. | 409 | | E078 | Routing update is not allowed as there is already a routing defined in the future. | 400 | | E080 | List of proof of documents that you have provided does not match the list of proof of documents required. Please upload missing documents. | 400 | | E081 | Request payload is invalid. | 400 | | E082 | Country not available in your pricelist. | 400 | | E083 | Real time provisioning not possible. | 400 | | E084 | The format of the AreaCode is invalid. The Area Code can only contain digits and have a maximum length of 20 digits. | 400 | | E086 | Invalid value for parameter {attribute}. | 400 | | E100 | There is no order with this identifier in our system. Please correct the identifier. | 400 | | E103 | You are not allowed to edit the order. You can only edit order in status Created or Need more information. | 400 | | E106 | The order with this identifier is related to a product that is not yet handled by our API. | 400 | | E107 | Attributes OrderOptions are not valid. regularOrderAllowed attribute can not be set to false if backOrderAllowed is equal to true. | 400 | | E112 | Some documents that you have provided are under validation. They will be validated by BICS as soon as possible. | 400 | | E113 | Address does not match the specification applied on this order. | 400 | | E114 | There is no enough numbers in our stock for the given origin. If you want, there are {amount} available numbers. | 400 | | E119 | A previous task is still pending. Please try again later. | 409 | | E122 | No commercial agreement found. Please contact BICS. | 400 |

get.summary: Query our available stock for a given product in a country
get.description:
This method allows you to check if a given quantity of numbers are available in our stock for
 a given product in a country. In case we don’t have enough numbers in our stock, the method also
 gives you the quantity of numbers still available so that you know how many numbers you can order.

parameters.description: Filter stock by product

parameters.name: country

get.summary: Retrieve the list of numbers in your inventory.
get.description: The _/numbers_ method allows you to retrieve the list of numbers in your inventory. You can retrieve the full list of your numbers in one call or filter out your request by providing the product, country, location, area code, status and number. As a response you will receive information linked to the number(s) that would help you manage them at your convenience.

parameters.name: product
parameters.description: Filter your inventory by product.

schema.description: Filter your inventory by product.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Filter your inventory by country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format.

schema.description: Filter your inventory by country in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Filter your inventory by location. (Not applicable for product GMN).

schema.description: Filter your inventory by location. (Not applicable for product GMN).

parameters.name: areaCode
parameters.description: Filter your inventory by areaCode.

schema.description: Filter your inventory by areaCode.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter your inventory by status. Valid status values are _Active_ and _Deactivated_. _Active_ referring to numbers provisioned and _Deactivated_ referring to numbers disconnected.

schema.description: Filter your inventory by status. Valid status values are _Active_ and _Deactivated_. _Active_ referring to numbers provisioned and _Deactivated_ referring to numbers disconnected.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Filter your inventory by number. Exact match is applied.

schema.description: Filter your inventory by number. Exact match is applied.

parameters.name: activeServiceCode
parameters.description:
Filter your inventory by services. You can filter on multiple values. Possible values.
  • IC - Inbound Call
  • NOC - National Outbound Call
  • IOC - International Outbound Call
  • ES - Emergency Services
  • DS - Directory Services
  • IM - Inbound Messaging
  • NOM - National Outbound Messaging
  • IOM - International Outbound Messaging

get.summary: Retrieve the available and active services of a given number from the inventory.
get.description: The /numbers/{number}/services method allows you to retrieve the available and active services of a given number from your inventory. Services that are not available on the given number are not returned.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: A given number from your inventory

schema.description: A given number from your inventory

post.summary: Order easily single or multiple number(s) that we have in our stock.
post.description:
Order easily single or multiple number(s) that we have in our stock. The method _/order_ provisions the number on the fly. In other words, the number can be used immediately after the API call.

You just have to fill out in your request the product and the country. Location, area code, quantity of numbers and routing details can be provided to better fit your needs. The content type of the request must be _application/json_.

You can also provide as an input one or multiple specific number(s) that we have in stock. The list of available numbers can be retrieved by calling the method _/availablenumbers_.

For some countries, you also need to specify the reference of the address of the end subscriber. For more information about address requirements, please read the Specifications API section and section Address Management API.

In case there are not enough numbers in stock, you also have the opportunity to split order in two orders: A regular order composed of the numbers that are available in stock and a back order for the remaining numbers that our operational team will fulfill as soon as possible. You can mention the order options in your request to specify the type of orders that API can create for you.

requestBody.description: Criteria's to order and provision routings of bulk numbers.

400.description: Bad request, An issue occurred while processing the input parameters.

404.description: No available numbers in stock anymore.

get.description:
List of all locations where you can order Cloud Numbers. A location is represented by a name and an area code. For Cloud Numbers, location indicates in most cases a city the number belongs to. It is also used to specify if it is a national or a mobile number.

You can also retrieve the list of area codes available for International Freephone Numbers or Global Mobile Numbers by specifying the product.

Additionally, you can filter out your request by providing the country.

parameters.name: product
parameters.description: Filter by product. Valid product codes are IBN, ITFS and GMN. IBN referring to Cloud Number, ITFS referring to International Freephone Number and GMN referring to Global Mobile Numbers.

delete.summary: Delete address reference linked to number(s)
delete.description: The method _/numbers/addressReference_. You can retrieve the current address linked to a number by calling the method /numbers/{number}. You can update the address by calling the method _/numbers/addressReferences._ You can retrieve a list of your addresses by calling the method _/addresses._ If the address is mandatory by country specifications, you are not allowed to delete the address.

200.description: Address references deleted for given numbers.

400.description: Bad request, An issue occurred while processing the input parameters.

409.description: A previous task is still pending.

post.description: The _/routes_ method allows you to change your routing details for one or multiple number(s). You can provide for each access type (fixed, mobile and payphone) if relevant the PoP and routing number to use. You can retrieve the current routing by calling the _/numbers/{number}_ method and your PoP names by calling the method _/reference/pops_.

Order easily single number that we have in our stock. The method _/order_ provisions the number on the fly. In other words, the number can be used immediately after the API call.

You just have to fill out in your request the product and the country. Location, area code,  and routing details can be provided to better fit your needs.

The content type of the request must be application/json. For some countries, you also need to specify the reference of the address of the end subscriber. For more information about address requirements, please read the Specifications API section and section Address Management API.

In case there are not enough numbers in stock, you also have an opportunity to create an order that our operational team will fulfill as soon as possible. You can mention the order options in your request to specify the type of orders that API can create for you.

get.summary: Retrieve available numbers for specific product, country and location.
get.description: The _/availablenumbers_ method allows you to preview a list of numbers that we have in our stock. You can then pick and order one or multiple numbers out of the list by calling the order method. The numbers cannot be reserved hence there is no guarantee that the numbers will still be available when you place the order.

parameters.name: product
parameters.description: Filter our stock by product. Valid product codes are _IBN_, _ITFS_ and _GMN_. _IBN_ referring to Cloud Number, _ITFS_ referring to International Freephone Number and _GMN_ referring to Global Mobile Number.

endUserInfoValidation.description:
Set of attributes indicates the validation status of the end user information. Attribute address gives the
status of the address of the end user and attribute addressDocuments gives the status of the documents provided
against the country specifications.

code.description:
Code of the service
  • IC: Inbound Call
  • NOC: National Outbound Call
  • IOC: International Outbound Call
  • ES: Emergency Services
  • DS: Directory Services
  • IM: Inbound Messaging
  • NOM: National Outbound Messaging
  • IOM: International Outbound Messaging

active.description:
true: the service is active on your number
false: the service is available but not active on your number

routing.description: The routing configuration you want to apply for this number.

country.description: Country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format.

areaCode.description: Area code of the number according to the national numbering plan.

accessArea.description: Tariff and/or service differentiator within a country. It intervenes in the billing.

addressReference.description: Identifier of the address of the end subscriber. It must be used to order numbers in countries where address of the end subscriber is required.

profile.description: Parameter which allows to select an exact price definition. See pricelist method for more info.

location.description: A Location indicates in most cases the city the number belongs to. It is also used to specify if it is a national or a mobile number.

country.description: Country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format.

product.description: Valid product codes are IBN and ITFS. IBN referring to Cloud Numbers and ITFS referring to International Freephone.

areaCode.description: AreaCode will only be displayed if areaCode was specified in the request parameters.

quantity.description: The quantity of available numbers in stock which meet your request. 0 if there is currently no available stock.

location.description: Location indicates in most cases the city that is attached to the number according to the national numbering plan. Sometimes, it is used to indicate whether it is a national or a mobile number.

checkStock.description: Flag indicating if there are enough numbers in stock to meet your request.

pop.description: Point of Presence (PoP) refers to your access point to which the traffic is routed.

accessNetwork.description: The network of the calling party. Routing detail can differ per access network of the calling party.

crnValue.description: The value to be used by the translation rule of the crnType. Only applicable to crnTypes prefix, suffix and freetext.

customerRouting.description: This parameter allows you to specify routing information for the numbers you buy. You can differentiate calls based on access type/access network by defining a different customer routing number per access type/access network. For more info about the combination access type/access network, please consult the method Get Pricelist. If you want to define the same routing configuration for every combination access type/access network, you can omit the routing attributes Access Type and Access Network, and only specify crnType and crnValue. If you order multiple numbers at once (by providing the quantity parameter) the same routing will be applied to all numbers ordered.

number.description: The provisioned number for which you want to add/update the address.

country.description: Origin Country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format of the Access Number. The attribute is mandatory for update address reference of UIFN or MSC numbers.

addressReference.description: The reference of an address that you want to link to the number. The address linked to this reference must have the status ‘Accepted’.

country.description: Country code in ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 format.

product.description: Valid product codes are 'IBN', 'ITFS' and 'GMN'. IBN referring to Cloud Numbers, ITFS referring to International Freephone and GMN referring to Global Mobile Number.

areaCode.description: Optional parameter that indicates the area code of the number according to the national numbering plan. This attribute is not mandatory.

accessArea.description: Optional parameter allowing to select an exact price definition. See pricelist method for more info.

addressReference.description: Optional parameter allowing to specify the reference of the address you want to link to the Cloud Number. It must be used to order numbers in countries where address of the end subscriber is required.

profile.description: Optional parameter allowing to select an exact price definition. See pricelist method for more info.

location.description: Optional parameter that indicates in most cases the city that is attached to the number according to the national numbering plan. Sometimes, it is used to indicate whether it is a national or a mobile number. This attribute is not mandatory and can be filled in for Cloud Number (IBN) only.

myReference.description: Optional parameter that allows you to specify your own order reference.

realTimeProvisioning.description:
• true: numbers can be delivered in real time
  • false: numbers can not be delivered in real time
  • null: Not applicable for this address requirement type

proofOfDocumentsNonGeo.description:
• true: proofing of documents is mandatory for every order of national (non-geo) numbers
  • false: proofing of documents is optional for every order of national (non-geo) numbers
  • null: Not applicable for this address requirement type

proofOfDocuments.description:
• true: proofing of documents is mandatory for every order
  • false: proofing of documents is optional for every order
  • null: Not applicable for this address requirement type

phoneNumber.description:
• true: phone number is mandatory for this order according the specs
  • false: phone number is not mandatory for this order according the specs

documentTypesForIndividual.description: List of required documents for individual

documents.description: list of documents needed for address requirements. See documentTypesForIndividual and documentTypesForCompany

documentTypesForCompany.description: List of required documents for Company

type.description:
• "Not required" : no address required
  • "Required in region" : address mandatory within region
  • "Required in country" : address mandatory within country
  • "Required worldwide" : address mandatory worldwide

vatNumber.description:
• true: VAT number is mandatory for this order according the specs
  • false: VAT number is not mandatory for this order according the specs
  • null: Not applicable for this address requirement type

Access type of the calling party. You can apply a different routing per access type. The possible values are
  • fix: Fix line

  • pay: Payphone line
  • fixMob: Fix and Mobile lines
  • fixPay: Fix and Payphone lines
  • mobPay: Mobile and Payphone lines
  • fixMobPay: Fix, Mobile and Payphone lines

routing.description: This parameter allows you to specify routing information for the numbers you buy. You can differentiate calls based on access type/access network by defining a different customer routing number per access type/access network. For more info about the combination access type/access network, please consult the method Get Pricelist. If you want to define the same routing configuration for every combination access type/access network, you can omit the routing attributes Access Type and Access Network, and only specify crnType and crnValue. If you order multiple numbers at once (by providing the quantity parameter) the same routing will be applied to all numbers ordered.

profile.description: Tariff differentiator within a country. It intervenes in the billing.

orderStatus.description:
Status of the order. For real-time number provisioning, the order status starts as Provisioning and updates to Delivered once completed in backend systems.
  • Created: Order is created. BICS will fulfil it as soon as possible.
  • End user information under validation: BICS validates provided end user information.
  • On hold, need more information: Some information is missing such as proof of document. Attribute Comment gives additional information.
  • Being processed: Order is being processed. Numbers will be provisioned soon.
  • Provisioning: Numbers are currently being set up and are not yet available for use. Once provisioning is complete, the status will be updated to Delivered.
  • Delivered: Order is delivered, and numbers are provisioned.
  • Cancelled by you: Order cancelled by you.
  • Cancelled by BICS: Order cancelled by BICS.

  • "Not offered anymore" : product and country not in your pricelist, but there are active numbers in your inventory

backOrderAllowed.description: Optional parameter that indicates if MyNumbers API is allowed to create an order that will be fulfilled by our operational team. It occurs when there are no numbers available in our stock. By default, this attribute is set to False.

consecutiveNumbers.description: Optional parameter to indicate if consecutive numbers should be ordered. By default, this attribute is set to False.

firstNumberEndsWith.description: Optional parameter to specify the ending digits of the first consecutive number. Only digits are allowed, and it must be a maximum of 4 digits.

regularOrderAllowed.description: Optional parameter that indicates if MyNumbers API is allowed to create an order that will be fulfilled by our operational team. It occurs when manual end user information is required. By default, this attribute is set to False.

orderOptions.description: Optional parameters that allow you to specify the type of orders you want to get.

Time division element that differentiates the price based on the day and time of the call.
  • `STD` - Standard
  • `P` - Peak

crnType.description:
The translation rule for the customer routing number that you want to apply to the number.
  • `prefix` - To prefix the access number with the specified crnValue.
  • `suffix` - To suffix the access number with the specified crnValue.
  • `freetext` - To define your own customer routing number.
  • `copy` - To use the access number as your customer routing number.
  • `NA` - To bar incoming calls for this routing.
